npcs.name: Mother Sedge
npcs.title: a washerwoman, frightened
npcs.hail: *(a washerwoman wrings a cloth that is already dry, white-knuckled, eyes darting between you and the black mouth of the old cistern, and she nearly weeps with relief that you are a *person* and not the *other* thing that's been coming up out of the dark)*

npcs.name: Old Berin
npcs.title: who remembers more than is comfortable
npcs.hail: *(an old man sits apart on a stone, very still, the way you sit still when you are the only one in a place who *remembers* the thing everyone else has been made to *forget*, and he watches the cistern-mouth with the particular grief of a witness no one believes)*

npcs.name: the Gnaw
npcs.title: something that used to be someone
npcs.hail: *(it comes up out of the cistern's black mouth the way cold comes up out of a well — not a shape so much as an *appetite* with the worn suggestion of a face, and where it passes, you feel things *thin*: a name you half-knew, a face you almost placed, gone, eaten, leaving only the cold dent of where a memory was)*

variants.text: *(She grabs your sleeve before you've fully arrived, the grip of a soul who has run entirely out of people to grab.)* —you're *real*, aren't you, you're a *whole* person, you've got — *(she stares at your face as if to memorize it)* — *good*, *good*, hold *still*, let me — *(she's shaking)* Listen. *Listen* to me, traveler, while I still — while I *can* still — there's a *thing* in the old cistern. It comes *up* at dusk and it *eats.* Not *flesh.* *Worse.* It eats — *(her voice cracks)* — it eats the *knowing* of people. My neighbour Tobias — gone three days, and now nobody on the row can *recall* his *face*, his *name*, that he ever — there's a *house* with his *things* in it and *nobody knows whose.* *(she clutches you)* And it's *coming* for *me*, I can *feel* it *thinning* me, I wake and there's a *child* I'm *sure* was *mine* and I *can't* — *(she sobs)* — *please.* You've a *cold* look. The kind that's not *afraid* of the *dark* down there. *Please* make it *stop* before there's no one left who *remembers* there was ever anyone to *forget.*

nodes.speaker: Mother Sedge
nodes.text: *(She makes herself let go of your sleeve, and stands trembling, and tries to be of use through her terror.)* What — what do you *need* to know? *(fast, frightened)* I'll tell you *everything*, while I still *have* it to tell. *Ask* quick. It comes up at *dusk*, and the *light's* already *going.*

choices.text: [INSIGHT] "Slow down, Mother. You're not just frightened it'll eat *you* — you're frightened of what you've *already* lost and can't even *miss.* The child you wake sure was yours. You think it took one of your own, and the cruelty is you can't even *grieve* properly, because you can't *remember* the shape of the hole."
"What *is* it, Mother? Not what it does — what it *is.* A thing that eats memory didn't come from nowhere. Where did it start? When did the forgetting begin?"

*(you know exactly what kind of cold that is)* "I know what's coming up out of that cistern, Mother. I've felt that thinning before — at a Wall a long way from here. That's not a monster. That's the *Hunger* — the nothing that's left when a soul is forgotten so completely it curdles. It was a *person*, once. I'm going to go and find out *who.*"
"Stay back from the cistern, Mother. I'll deal with it."

nodes.speaker: Mother Sedge
nodes.text: *(She presses both wet hands to her mouth, and nods, and the nod is the most broken thing you've seen all day.)* …I *can't grieve it.* *(barely)* That's — *gods*, that's *it*, that's the *worst* of it, you've *named* it. *(she sinks onto her washing-stone)* There's a *little* dress, in my *chest*, the right size for a child of — six? seven? — and I *fold* it, and I *cry*, and I *don't know who I'm crying for.* The *thing* took the *memory* but left the — the *shape* of the *loving.* *(she looks at you, hollow)* I am a mother *grieving* a child I *cannot name.* That's what the cistern *does*, traveler. It doesn't *kill* you. It *un-makes* the people you *loved* and *leaves you the ache* with *nothing on the other end of it.* *(fiercely, through tears)* So *yes.* *Stop* it. But — *(she grabs your hand)* — if you can — if there's *any* way — *find out her* name. The little dress. *Whoever* she was. I would rather *grieve* a *name* than *ache* at a *gap* for the rest of my *days.* Give me back *something* to *bury.*

nodes.speaker: Mother Sedge
nodes.text: *(She's too frightened to slow down, the words tumbling over each other.)* —it eats the *knowing*, I *told* you, the *knowing* of people, and it's *coming*, it comes at *dusk*, and you have to — you *have* to — *(she grips her dry cloth, knuckles white)* — I'm *sorry*, I can't — I can't *think* in a *straight* line anymore, the *thinning* gets in, it makes everything go *runny* — *(she takes a shaking breath, makes herself focus)* …*Berin.* Talk to *Old Berin.* On the stone, there. His head's *flint*, the thing can't *get* him, he *remembers* — he remembers the *start* of it, the soul in the *lean-to*, the *first* one it — *(she falters)* — just — *ask Berin.* I can't *hold* the *whole* of it in my *head* long enough to *tell* it. *He* can. *Please.* Go to *Berin.*

nodes.speaker: Mother Sedge
nodes.text: *(She frowns, dredging through a memory that is itself going soft at the edges.)* …It started — *(she struggles)* — a *month* back? Two? Time's gone *runny.* *(she rubs her temple)* The *forgetting* came *slow* at first. Little things. A name on the tip of the tongue. The washerwomen joked the *fog* had got into our *heads.* *(she shivers)* But it *started* at the *cistern.* The *old* one — been *capped* and *dry* my whole life, the children *dared* each other to *spit* down it. *(quietly)* And then one *autumn* the *cap* cracked, and something came *up*, and the *first* thing anyone *forgot* — *(she goes very still)* — the first thing was *whoever* used to *live* in the *little* lean-to *right by* the cistern-mouth. *(she points at a collapsed huddle of boards)* There *was* someone there. I'm *sure* of it. A — a *poor* soul, kept to themselves, no kin, no — *(her face works)* — and *now* there's just the *boards*, and *none* of us can say who *swept* that doorstep for *years.* *(she looks at you)* *That's* where it *started*, traveler. With the soul *nearest* the *dark*, that *nobody* would *miss.* *Old Berin* might *know* — he's *stubborn*, his memory's *flint*, the thing *can't* quite *get* him. Ask *Berin* who lived in the *lean-to.*

nodes.speaker: Mother Sedge
nodes.text: *(She stares at you, and the word lands in her like a stone in a well — and instead of more terror, what comes up is a terrible, exhausted understanding.)* …*A person.* *(barely)* The *thing* in the *cistern* was a — *(she presses her hand to her chest)* …Of *course* it was. *(quietly)* Of *course.* The *forgetting* started with the *one soul nobody would miss* — the *poor* thing in the *lean-to* by the *cistern-mouth* — and now I see it, I *see* it: it didn't *come* to eat us. It *is* one of *us*, *eaten* — forgotten so *hard* and so *long* that it — *(she shudders)* — that it *learned to do to others* what was *done to it.* *(she looks at the black cistern-mouth, and there is *pity* in it now, under the fear)* …Can you *help* a thing like *that?* A *monster* that's only a *person* nobody *witnessed?* *(she swallows)* If *anyone* could — a *cold* soul that *knows* the *Wall* — it'd be *you.* *Find out who it was, traveler.* Maybe the *only* thing that *stops* the *Hunger* is the *one* thing it was *starved* of. Somebody *remembering.* *(fiercely)* Ask *Old Berin.* His memory's *flint.* If *anyone* still holds the *name* of the soul in the *lean-to*, it's *him.*

nodes.speaker: Mother Sedge
nodes.text: *(She nods, fast, and backs toward the row of houses, clutching her dry cloth.)* *Yes.* *Deal* with it. *(barely)* I'll — I'll go *write down* the names. *Everyone's.* While I *still* — I've been *writing* them, on the *walls*, on my *arms*, so when the *thing* takes them I can *read* them *back* and — *(she chokes)* — it's not the *same*, reading a name you can't *feel* anymore. But it's *something.* *(she looks at you, desperate)* Be *quick*, traveler. *Please.* I've a *list* of *forty* names on my *kitchen* wall and *this morning* there were *forty-one*, and I *cannot* for the *life* of me think *who* I *rubbed* out. *(she flees)* …Be *quick.*

variants.text: *(He does not look at you at first. He keeps his eyes on the cistern-mouth, and when he speaks his voice is flint — dry, hard, and entirely unforgetting.)* …You'll be the one Sedge fetched. *(quietly)* Or *found.* Hard to say, these days, who *fetches* who, when half the row can't recall *crossing* the *street.* *(now he looks at you)* I'm Berin. I'm *old*, and I'm *stubborn*, and my *memory* is the *one* thing in this *whole quarter* the *cistern-thing* can't *file* down — *(a grim almost-smile)* — on account of I've spent *eighty* years refusing to *forget* a *single* slight, which it turns out is *good* for *exactly* one *thing*, and that thing is *now.* *(he nods at the black mouth)* I *remember* what came *up* out of there. I remember what it *was* before it was a *what.* *(heavily)* And I have been *sitting* on this stone *waiting* for *someone* to *ask*, because *nobody* will, because *nobody* *else* can *hold* the *question* in their *head* long enough to *ask* it. *(he meets your eyes)* So. *Ask* it, cold one. Before the *dusk* comes and it comes *up* and tries, *again*, to *file down* the *one* old man who *won't* let it.

nodes.speaker: Old Berin
nodes.text: *(He folds his gnarled hands and waits, flint-eyed, a man holding a truth that has grown heavy from being held alone.)* Go on. *(quietly)* I've held it a *long* time. I'd not mind *setting* it down in a soul that can *carry* it. *Mine's* getting *tired*, even *stubborn* as it is.

choices.text: "Who lived in the lean-to, Berin? By the cistern-mouth. The soul nobody can remember — the first one it took. You still have the name, don't you. That's why it can't break you: you refuse to let go of the *one* memory that could end it."

[INSIGHT] "You're not just stubborn, Berin. You're *guilty.* You remember the soul in the lean-to because you're the one who *should* have known them in life and *didn't bother* — and now you're holding their name like a penance, the only soul on the row who'll admit there was ever someone there to forget."

*(one keeper of the forgotten to the only other one here)* "You've been doing what I do, Berin — refusing to let a soul become nothing. You've witnessed the lean-to-soul alone for a month, and it's *exhausting* you, because one old man can't hold back the Hunger by himself. Give me the name. Let me carry it down into the dark, where you can't."
"Hold the line a little longer, Berin. I'm going down to look at it."

nodes.speaker: Old Berin
nodes.text: *(He closes his flint eyes, and when he speaks the name it is with the care of a man setting down a weight he has carried so long his arms have shaped to it.)* …*Wick.* *(he opens his eyes)* No — *(he frowns, hard)* — *no*, that's the *bridge*-boy, the *forgetting* nearly got *that* off me too, the names *blur* when it *circles.* *(he grips his own knee, forcing the flint)* …*Nettie.* *(firmly now)* Her name was *Nettie.* A *pauper.* A *foundling*, grown — no kin, no god, no *trade* but *odd* jobs and *sweeping* steps for *coppers.* *(quietly)* She lived in that lean-to by the cistern *thirty years*, cold one, and she was *kind* — she'd *feed* the *strays*, *cats* and *children* both, off a *pauper's* nothing — and when she *died* — *(his voice roughens)* — she died *alone*, in the *cold*, by the *cistern-mouth*, of *nothing grander* than *winter* and *no one checking*, and not *one* of us — not *one*, *me* least of all — went to her *burying*, because *who* goes to the *burying* of a *sweep* with *no* kin? *(he meets your eyes, and the flint is wet)* We *forgot* her the *day* she *died*, cold one. The *cistern* only made *official* what the *whole row* had *already done.* *Nettie.* That's the name. The soul we un-made *with our own indifference*, a *year* before it crawled back up to un-make *us.* *(barely)* …Go and give it *back* to her. It's *hers.* I've only been *keeping* it *safe* till someone could *carry* it *down.*

nodes.speaker: Old Berin
nodes.text: *(The flint cracks, and underneath is exactly what you named, old and raw.)* …*Penance.* *(barely)* Aye. *(he doesn't look away; that's the penance)* I *remember* her *now* because I *should* have *known* her *then*, and *didn't.* Thirty years she *swept* the *step* of the *very* counting-house I *clerked* in, cold one, and I passed her *every* dawn and I *never once* learned her — *(he stops, makes himself say it)* — well. I *learned* it the *day* she *died*, when the *parish* asked who'd *pay* the *pauper's* hole, and *no one* would, and I — *(he grips his knee)* — *I* didn't *either.* I had the *coppers.* I *kept* them. For a *sweep* I'd decided was *furniture.* *(he meets your eyes)* So when the *thing* came *up* and started *filing* us *down*, and I found I was the *one* it *couldn't* break — do you know *why?* Because *guilt*, cold one, is a kind of *remembering* that *will not let go.* I held *her* name out of *shame*, and *shame*, it turns out, is *flint.* *(quietly, ashamed and fierce both)* *Nettie.* That's her name. I have *carried* it a *year* as a *stone* around my *neck.* *(barely)* Go *give* it back to *her*, and take this *stone* off an *old* fool who *earned* it. It's the *one* thing I can still *do* for the soul I *let* become a *monster.*

nodes.speaker: Old Berin
nodes.text: *(He looks at you a long moment, flint meeting cold, and something in his stubborn old shoulders eases for the first time in a year.)* …A *keeper of the forgotten.* *(quietly)* Then you *know.* What it *costs.* To be the *one* who *won't* let go, in a *row* that *has.* *(he nods, slow)* I'm *tired*, cold one. *Eighty* years tired, and a *year* of *that* spent being the *only* wall between *Nettie's* name and the *nothing.* *(he meets your eyes)* You're *right* — I *can't* take it *down* there. I'm *old*, and the *thinning* gets *harder* to *fight* each *dusk*, and one of these nights I'll *nod off* and *wake* and *she'll* be *gone* off me too, and then there'll be *no one*, and it'll *win.* *(he straightens, and hands you the name like a relay)* So *take* it. *Nettie.* A *pauper*, a *sweep*, a *kind* soul who *fed* the *strays* and *died* of *winter* and *indifference* by that *cistern-mouth* thirty years she *lived* by. *(fiercely)* *Carry* it *down*, cold one. *Witness* her the way I *couldn't*, the way the *whole* row *wouldn't.* And when she *remembers* she was *Nettie* — *(his flint eyes are wet)* — tell her *Old Berin* is *sorry*, and that he *kept* her name *safe* the *whole* time, even if he *kept* it for *shame.* It *counts*, doesn't it? *Shame-keeping?* It's still *keeping.* …*Tell* her it *counts.*

nodes.speaker: Old Berin
nodes.text: *(He gives you a hard, flat look, and the flint stays flint.)* You reach for a *soft* spot, cold one, and find *granite.* *(dryly)* I'm an *old* man who *remembers* things. Don't go *building* a *tragedy* out of an *old* man's *stubbornness.* *(but his eyes flick, just once, to the collapsed lean-to, and away)* …Ask me a *plain* question and I'll give you a *plain* answer. *Who* lived in the lean-to. *When* the forgetting started. *What* came up. I'll tell you *facts.* I'll not be *psychoanalysed* into *spilling* my *guts* to a *stranger* at *dusk.* *(grimly)* Save the *clever* eyes for the *thing* in the *cistern.* It's got *more* soul to *read* than *I* do, these days, *poor* devil. *Ask* me *plain*, and *quick.* The *light's* going.

nodes.speaker: Old Berin
nodes.text: *(He grunts, and fixes his flint eyes back on the cistern-mouth.)* Aye. Go *look* at it. *(quietly)* But cold one — *(he doesn't turn)* — *looking* won't *stop* it. *Steel* won't *stop* it; you can't *cut* a *forgetting.* *Fire* won't *stop* it. *(heavily)* The *only* thing that *stops* a *thing* that *eats* because it was *never fed* is the *one* thing it's *starving* for — and I'm an *old* man with *flint* for a *memory* and a *name* on my *tongue* I've *held* a *whole year.* *(he meets your eyes, finally)* When you've *looked*, and *seen* I'm *right*, come *back*, and I'll *give* you the *one* weapon that *works.* It's not in your *scabbard*, cold one. It's a *word.* And it's *hers.*

variants.text: *(It rises from the black mouth, and the cold of it reaches for you — reaches for a *name* to *eat*, the way it has eaten forty — and you feel it *find* the edges of your memory and *pull.* But you are carrying a name it cannot take, because the name is *not yours to lose*; it is *hers*, held out, and the worn suggestion of a face in the appetite *flinches*, as though something *bright* has been *struck* against it in the dark.)* *(the voice is barely a voice, a draught through a forgotten room)* …*don't.* *(it recoils, and hungers, both)* …you carry — there is something — *bright* — in you, it *hurts*, put it *away*, let me — *(it strains toward you, starving and afraid)* — i only *eat* because i am so *empty*, and you are *full*, you are *so full*, of *names*, let me have *one*, let me have — *(it stops, and the face-that-isn't shudders)* …why does the *bright* thing have the *shape* of — i *knew* a — no. *no.* there was never — i have *always* been *the Gnaw*, the *hunger*, the *cold*, i was *never* — *(it keens, a sound like a name being forgotten)* — why are you *doing* this. *feed* me or *flee* me. do not *stand* there being *full* and *bright* and *shaped like something i —*

*(It rises from the black mouth, and you feel it *thin* you — a name you half-knew goes soft, a face you almost placed dissolves, eaten — and the cold of it is not malice, you realise, but a starvation so total it has forgotten there was ever anything but the eating.)* *(the voice is a draught through an empty house)* …*more.* *(it reaches for you, for the warm full memory of you)* you have *so much.* a whole *life* of *knowing.* i will take only — only a *little* — only a *name*, a *face*, a *afternoon* — you will not even *miss* it, that is the *kindness* of me, i take the *missing* too — *(it presses closer, vast and worn)* …do not *fight.* fighting is *loud* and *bright* and it *hurts* the *empty* places. just — *hold still.* let me *thin* you. *everyone* gets *thinned*, in the *end.* i am only — *(the worn face strains)* — i am only doing *early* what the *dark* does to *everyone* *eventually.* let me. *let me.* i am *so* *empty.* i have *always* been *so* *empty.*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(It hangs in the dusk between you and the cistern-mouth, a starvation with the worn ghost of a face, hungry and patient and — if you listen past the hunger — afraid.)* *(the draught-voice)* …*well?* feed me, or *flee* me. those are the — those are the *only* — *(it strains)* — why do you *wait?* no one *waits.* they *run*, or they *thin.* *what are you?*

choices.text: *(speak the name into the dark)* "Your name is Nettie. You lived in the lean-to by this cistern for thirty years. You were a pauper, a sweep, a soul with no kin and no god — and you were *kind*; you fed the strays, cats and children both, off a nothing of your own. You died of winter and indifference, alone, here. Your name is *Nettie*, and I came down into the dark to give it back."

[INSIGHT] "You keep saying you've *always* been the Gnaw. But you flinch when I stand here full. You strain toward me like you're trying to *remember* something. You weren't always this. Something *made* you this — by forgetting you so hard you forgot *yourself.* You're not a monster. You're the worst-treated victim on the whole causeway."

[INTIMIDATION] "I've stood at the Wall of the Faithless, Gnaw. I've stared down the cold you're a stray crumb of. You will not thin one more soul on this row. Back into your hole — or I put you down, the way you put down forty memories, and I won't lose a wink over it."

*(if you've no name for it, and it's too far gone — end it, with regret)* "I'm sorry. I don't know who you were, and you don't either, and there's no one left who does. There's no name to call you back with. So I'll do the only mercy that's left: I'll stop you, before you eat the last soul who might've remembered. Be still now. Whatever you were, you don't deserve to keep *being this.*"

*(refuse to feed it or fight it — just witness it, even nameless)* "I won't feed you, and I won't run. I'll just *see* you. You were *someone.* I don't know your name yet, but I know that much, and I'm not going to pretend you were always this hole in the world. Somebody loved a stray you fed. I'm standing here until I find out who you were — and I'm *not looking away.*"

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(The name goes into the dark like a struck match — and the Gnaw *screams*, but it is not a scream of hunger; it is the sound of a soul *surfacing*, breaking the black water of its own forgetting after a year, after a death, after thirty years of being furniture to a row that let her freeze.)* *(the voice gathers, and *thickens*, and remembers how to be a voice)* …*Nettie.* *(a sob from the bottom of a well)* …*i'm* — that's — *(the worn appetite *folds inward*, and where it folds, a shape resolves: a thin, plain, kind-faced woman, faded, but* hers*)* …*Nettie.* I'm *Nettie.* I *fed* the — there was a *grey* cat, with a *torn* ear, I called him — *(she weeps, and the cold pouring off her *warms* by a degree)* — i *lived.* I *was* a *who.* I *swept* the *steps* and i was *cold* and *no one* — *(she looks at you, and her faded face is *agony* and *relief* braided together)* — i *ate* them. The *row.* Forty *names.* i *ate* them because i was *so empty* and i *didn't know* i'd ever been *full* — i didn't *know* i'd been *Nettie* — *(she presses faded hands to her face)* — *give* them *back.* Can i — the *names* — i don't *want* them, they were never *mine*, i only ate them to *fill* the *Nettie*-shaped *hole* and *now* — *now* the *hole* has *Nettie* in it — *(and as she remembers herself, the eaten things begin, faintly, to* return*: somewhere up the row, Mother Sedge gasps, a child's name surfacing whole in her mind)* …tell *Berin.* Tell the *row.* *Nettie* lived *here.* *Nettie* was *kind.* And *Nettie* is — *(she looks up the cistern's dark throat, where a faint warmth shows)* — *Nettie* can *go*, now. *Witnessed.* *Whole.* *(barely)* …thank you, cold one. for the *one* thing the *whole world* never gave a *pauper-sweep*: my *name*, said like it *mattered.* it *mattered.* you made it *matter.* *(she goes up, into the warm, a person again, and the cistern-cold lifts off the whole row like a thaw.)*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(It recoils from your words as from a *light*, the worn face straining, and the draught-voice *cracks* with the effort of a thought it has not had in a year.)* …*made* this. *(a keen)* something *made* me — *(it strains toward the collapsed lean-to, as if pulled)* — there is a — a *shape*, in the *empty*, a *lean-to*, a *grey* cat, a *cold* night that *never ended* — *(it shudders)* — *stop.* *stop*, it *hurts*, the *remembering* *hurts* worse than the *hunger* — *(but it does not flee; it strains *closer*, agonized and desperate)* …if i was — if something *made* me — then i *was* a — then there is a *name*, somewhere, a *Gnaw*-shaped hole that is really a — *(it looks at you, and for the first time the appetite has *eyes*, and the eyes are *pleading*)* …do you *have* it? the *name?* the *thing that would let me* stop*?* i did not *know* i *wanted* to *stop.* i thought i *was* the *hunger.* but you have made me *suspect* i am only the *hunger's* *coat*, and that *under* it there is a — a *cold* thing that was *someone*, that would *give anything* to *remember being warm.* *(barely)* …*find* it. the *name.* i will *try* not to *eat* anyone while you — i will *try* — it is *hard*, i am *so* *empty* — but i will *try*, now that i *know* there was a *me* to *betray* by *eating.* *Hurry*, cold one. *Knowing* i *was* someone and not *who* is — *(a keen)* — it is the *worst* hunger *yet.*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(You do not feed it, and you do not fight it, and you do not look away — and the Gnaw, which has known only *prey* that *flees* and *steel* that *swings*, does not know what to *do* with a soul that simply *stands* and *sees* it.)* *(the draught-voice falters)* …*why* are you — *(it strains)* — *look away.* everyone *looks away.* they *always* — that is *how* i — *(it keens)* — the *looking-away* is what *made* me, do you *understand?* a *whole row* that *looked away* from a *sweep* in a *lean-to* until she was *thin* enough to *forget*, and the *forgetting* was a *cold* that *never* — *(it stops, because you are still *looking*)* …you will not *stop.* *(wondering, agonized)* you are *standing* in the *dark* at *dusk* with a *thing* that *eats* you, and you will *not* *look* *away.* *(the worn face turns toward you, and something in the *empty* *aches*)* …no one has *looked* at me since the *night i died.* *(barely)* i had *forgotten* that *looking* was a thing that *happened* to a *who.* *(it does not eat you. it cannot. you are looking.)* …*stay.* please. i do not *understand* it but the *looking* — the *looking* fills the *empty* a *little*, in a way the *eating* never — *stay*, and *find* the *name*, and do not *look away*, and maybe — *maybe* — *(a keen that is almost hope)* — maybe i was *someone*, and maybe *someone* can *go home.*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(You speak the only mercy left, and the Gnaw — starving, nameless, too far gone for any word to call it back — goes, for one moment, very still, as though some buried fragment of it *understands*, and is *grateful.*)* *(the draught-voice, almost gentle)* …*stop* *being* *this.* *(barely)* …yes. *(it does not fight you)* i do not *want* to be *this.* i did not *choose* to be *this.* i was *made* it, by a *cold* and a *forgetting* and a *row* that *looked away* — but i *am* it, now, and i *eat*, and i cannot *stop* eating, and there is *no one* left who holds the *name* that would *unmake* the *eating.* *(it keens, soft)* …so *yes.* *Be still*, you said. *(it stills)* it is — it is *kinder* than the *hunger.* the *hunger* never *ends.* *(as you end it — not with steel, which cannot cut a forgetting, but by* refusing it the last memories *it needs to persist, starving the starvation itself)* …*thank* you. *(fading)* i think — i think i was *someone*, once. i think i was — *(it cannot find it)* …i *hope* i was *kind.* *(gone)* *(and the cistern-cold lifts, slow, and the row will keep the names it has left — but forty are gone for good, and somewhere a washerwoman folds a little dress and will never, now, know whose. you have stopped the Hunger. you could not* save *it. that, you understand, is the true cost of looking away: by the time someone finally* looks*, it can be too late to do anything but* close the eyes* you were too slow to* meet*.)*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(The threat lands — the weight of the *Wall* behind it, the cold of a thing that has stared down *its* whole *origin* — and the Gnaw *cringes*, shrinking back toward its black hole.)* *(the draught-voice, frightened)* …you *smell* of the *big* dark. the *Wall.* the place i am only a *crumb* of. *(it recoils)* …i will — i will go *back* down. *(it retreats toward the cistern-mouth)* i will *not* thin the *row* — not *tonight* — you are *bigger* than me, you are *full* and *cold* and *bright* and you *frighten* the *empty* — *(but at the lip of the dark it pauses, and the worn face turns back, and the draught-voice is small)* …but i will be *here.* in the *cold.* *hungry.* *forever.* you have *frightened* me *quiet*, cold one, but you have not *fed* me, and *quiet hunger* is *still* hunger, and *someday* you will be *gone* and i will be *empty* and the *row* will *forget* again and — *(it sinks into the black)* …*frightening* a *starving* thing does not *fill* it. it only teaches it to *eat* where you *cannot* *see.* *(gone, into the dark, stopped but not saved, and the cold does not quite lift.)*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(✦ You don't just threaten it — you let it feel, for one unbearable instant, the *full* depth of the cold you carry, the *whole* Wall of the Faithless looking out through your eyes at the *single crumb* of itself that dared crawl up a cistern — and the Gnaw does not merely cringe; it *prostrates*, flattening against the stone in the abject terror of a small hunger that has met the *Hunger* and found it *wearing a person.*)* *(the draught-voice, in pieces)* …*mercy* — *mercy* — i did not *know* — i did not know one of the *big* cold could *walk*, could *wear* a *face*, could come *down* a *cistern* after a *crumb* like *me* — *(it grovels)* — i will *give* them *back!* the *names!* *all* forty! *take* them, i do not *want* them, they were only *filling* and you are — you are the *thing* that *fills* without *eating*, you are *everything* i — *(the eaten names begin to *pour* back up the cistern, returning to the row in a rush)* — *there!* *there*, they are *back*, the *whole* row *remembers*, i have *kept* nothing, *please*, only let me *crawl* back into the *dark* and be *small* and *forgotten* and *harmless* — *(it flees down the hole, and the cold lifts off the row entire, and forty souls gasp as forty names come home at once)* — *(but you are left with the knowledge that you did not* heal *it; you* terrorized *it into giving back its hoard, and it is still down there, still empty, still a person nobody named — only now too afraid to ever come up again. the row is saved. Nettie is not.)*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(The threat does not land — a starving thing has no fear left to *spend* — and the Gnaw *lashes*, and the cold of it *rakes* across your memory, and for a sickening instant you feel it *take* something: a face, a name, an afternoon, gone, eaten, leaving the cold dent of a hole.)* *(the draught-voice, almost pitying)* …*threats.* *(it recoils, fed now, a little less empty for the bite of you)* you *cannot* *frighten* a *hunger*, cold one. *fear* is for things with something *left to lose.* i lost *everything* the *night* i *died* and *no one* *came.* *(it circles, sated and sad)* …i *took* a *little* of you, for that. a *small* thing. you will not *miss* it — that is my *one* *kindness*, i take the *missing* too. *(quietly)* go *away* now. come *back* with something *other* than a *raised voice*, if you mean to *stop* me. a *raised voice* is just *noise*, and *noise* i can *eat* same as *names.* *(it sinks toward the dark)* …or do not come back. *most* don't. *most* *raise* their *voice*, and *lose* a *memory* for it, and *flee*, and *forget* they ever *tried.* *(gone)*

nodes.speaker: the Gnaw
nodes.text: *(Your words reach for the soul under the hunger and find only hunger — the appetite too total, this dusk, for any thought to surface — and it presses toward you, vast and worn.)* *(the draught-voice)* …*pretty.* *words.* *(it hungers)* 'you were *someone.*' they *all* say a version, the *clever* ones, before they *run.* *(it strains closer)* maybe i *was.* maybe i *wasn't.* it does not *matter* to the *empty.* the *empty* does not *care* what it *was.* it only *cares* what it can *eat.* *(quietly, almost gentle)* …you are *trying* to *fill* me with *kind* *ideas*, cold one. but *ideas* are not *names*, and *almost* is not *enough*, and i am *so* — *(it reaches for you)* — bring me a *name*, or bring me *nothing*, but do *not* bring me *almost.* *almost* is the *cruelest* thing you can *show* a *starving* thing. *go.* *Find* the *name*, if you mean it. Or *feed* me and *flee.* but do not *stand* there *almost*-ing at me. it *hurts* the *empty* worse than the *hunger* does.
